Our Streamlined Drone Registration Process in Thailand At MyDroneThailand , we’ve simplified the complex drone registration process, handling all bureaucratic formalities with Thai authorities on your behalf. Here’s how our efficient service works: 1. Dual Authority Registration We manage applications with two key Thai agencies: CAAT (Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand) : For airspace usage permission NBTC (National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission) : For radio control approval 2. Document Preparation To initiate your registration, we require: Drone specifications A few essential documents (we’ll guide you on specifics) Photos of your drone 3. Insurance Coverage We provide mandatory liability insurance that meets CAAT legal requirements, ensuring you’re fully compliant. 4. Swift Processing Our standard process takes less than 1 week Expedited approvals available in some cases Start the process anytime before your trip 5. Download the UAS App to your smartphone Once you’re in Thailand, you’ll need to register your flights through the UAS app. No worries: we’ll send you an easy step-by-step guide. This way, you can take off legally and stress-free. Here is a first look 6. Final Step of registration in Thailand The last step is completed upon your arrival in Thailand, ensuring a smooth transition from registration to flying. Why Choose Our Service? We offer a competitive price. Mandatory Insurance Options : 699 / 899 THB Thailand requires third-party liability insurance for all registered drones. Our rates are based on drone size: • 699 THB for a drone up to 300g • 899 THB for a drone between 301 and 3000g Note: If you have qualifying international drone insurance, you may not need additional coverage. However, Thai insurance often simplifies the process. We handle the complexities, so you can focus on capturing stunning aerial footage of Thailand’s beauty. the procedure 4 Easy Steps to Register Your Drone in Thailand At MyDroneThailand , we’ve streamlined the drone registration process into four simple steps. Here’s a detailed guide to help you navigate through each stage efficiently: STEP 1 – Initiate Your Application • Complete Our Online Form – Click here to start your application (Provide essential details about you and your drone) • After form submission, we’ll email you a list of necessary documents (Typically includes Drone photos and specifications) Pro Tip: Have clear, high-resolution photos of your drone ready to expedite the process. STEP 2 – Insurance and Payment • If needed, we’ll send you an insurance application (Review and sign the document electronically) • Make the Payment (We’ll provide secure payment instructions) Note: If you have qualifying international drone insurance, let us know to potentially save on insurance costs. STEP 3 – CAAT Approval Process • Upon arrival in Thailand, you’ll receive a code from CAAT on your Thai phone number (Send us this code promptly) OR choose our Thai phone service (400 THB) to avoid this step Temporarily unavailable, instead we recommend to buy an eSIM with phone number (data / calls / text) that can be activated once you arrive • We’ll complete the CAAT registration process – You’ll receive official CAAT approval for drone operation Time-Saving Tip: Our Thai phone service can significantly smooth out this step, especially for first-time visitors. Temporarily unavailable STEP 4 – NBTC Registration Completion • Send us a photo of your immigration stamp upon entering Thailand (This confirms your presence in the country for NBTC records) • We finalize your NBTC registration + You receive official NBTC approval, completing your drone registration Warning NBTC offices will be closed on Songkran from 10. -15. April. Remember: Both CAAT and NBTC approvals are necessary for legal drone operation in Thailand. Previous Submit form Frequently Asked Questions About Drone Registration in Thailand Got questions about registering your drone in Thailand? We’ve got answers! Here are some of the most common queries we receive: Do I need to register my drone in Thailand? Yes, all drones with cameras must be registered in Thailand, regardless of weight. This includes popular models like the DJI Mini series, which are under 250g. You need approvals from both CAAT (Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand) and NBTC (National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission). How long does the drone registration process take? With our service, the entire process typically takes less than a week before you enter Thailand. However, the final steps can only be completed upon your arrival in Thailand. We recommend starting the process at least 2 weeks before your trip to ensure smooth completion. Warning NBTC offices will be closed on Songkran from 11. -15. April. Can I register my drone after I arrive in Thailand? While it’s possible, we strongly recommend starting the process before your arrival. This allows us to prepare most of the paperwork in advance, saving you valuable vacation time. The final steps, including receiving CAAT approval, must be done after you enter Thailand. What documents do I need for drone registration in Thailand? A copy of your passport Photos of your drone (from various angles) Drone specifications (model, weight, serial number) Proof of insurance (we can assist with this) We’ll guide you through submitting these documents once you start your application. Do I need insurance for my drone in Thailand? Yes, third-party liability insurance is mandatory for all registered drones in Thailand. We offer insurance options tailored to different drone sizes, ranging from 599 to 799 THB. If you have qualifying international drone insurance, it may be accepted, but Thai insurance often simplifies the process. Can I fly my drone anywhere in Thailand once it’s registered? Even with registration, there are still restrictions on where you can fly. Drones are prohibited in many national parks, near airports, and in some urban areas. In the mandatory UAS app, you can always check where flights are allowed. There you’ll see possible restrictions at your current location and across the country. In addition, we’ll explain how you can apply for a permit to fly in national parks. What happens if I fly an unregistered drone in Thailand? Flying an unregistered drone in Thailand can result in severe penalties, including fines up to 100,000 THB (approximately $3,000 USD) and/or imprisonment. Your drone may also be confiscated. It’s crucial to complete the registration process to avoid these risks. Do I need a Thai phone number for drone registration? Yes, a Thai phone number is required to complete the CAAT registration process. You also need a Thai number to be able to download the UAS app. We recommend YESIM to get a Thai phone number and use internet in Thailand. Installed in minutes. Share the wireless mobile internet with other mobile device. This eSIM can be used in over 200 countries. How much does drone registration cost in Thailand? Our comprehensive service costs 2,500 THB, which includes handling both CAAT and NBTC registrations. Additional costs include mandatory insurance (699-899 THB depending on drone size) and an optional phone number service (500 THB). The total cost typically ranges from 3,199 to 3,899 THB. Can I use my drone for commercial purposes in Thailand after registration? The standard registration we provide is for recreational use. If you plan to use your drone for commercial purposes, additional permits and qualifications may be required. Contact us for more information on commercial drone use in Thailand. Still have questions? Don’t hesitate to contact us for personalized assistance with your drone registration in Thailand! Scroll to Top
